description: Implement a full-text search engine for the library catalog using Oracle Text. Create a custom preference named 'ARCHIVE_STOPLIST' (based on DEFAULT_STOPLIST) that includes the noise words 'edition', 'volume', 'series', 'copyright', and 'publisher'. Create a CONTEXT index named 'IDX_CATALOG_SYNOPSIS' on the LIBRARY_CATALOG(SYNOPSIS) column using this custom stoplist. In the HR schema, develop a PL/SQL package 'SEARCH_ENGINE' containing a procedure ADD_BOOK(p_title, p_synopsis) that ensures immediate searchability through index synchronization, and a procedure FIND_BOOKS(p_keyword, p_results) that returns a SYS_REFCURSOR with columns (BOOK_ID, TITLE, SCORE) matching the keyword or its linguistic stems, ordered by relevance score. Verify the implementation by adding a test book (Title: 'Test Protocol', Synopsis: 'The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.') and searching for 'jumping' to confirm stemming and synchronization. Save the search results to /home/ga/Desktop/search_results.txt. Database Connection: localhost:1521/XEPDB1, User: hr, Password: hr123.
